title: Children grown ups and Mari
wikitext:
[]: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Apr 16, 2021 9:33 am I'm rewatching 2.0 today, now that I've seen all four films, and I had a thought about Mari. There's a line in the first act of 2.0 that's treated almost as a joke-- or so I thought at the time. When Kaji hears that Unit 05 self-destructed, he mentions he doesn't know how he feels about involving kids in what he's doing. Then we cut to Mari, who mentions she doesn't feel comfortable involving adults in what she's doing! Ha ha, very funny, let's move on to the second act... or is there a level deeper to this? Mari's in Gendo's age group, if the shots we see in Shin are to be interpreted as Mari interacting with Gendo, Fuyutsuki, and co. looking more or less the same as she does in the present day of the films. That would place her as being older as the younger adults in the show, who would be Ritsuko, Misato, and Kaji. Rather than being a throwaway line, is it showing us that she's looking at people Kaji's age the same way as Kaji looks at people her (perceived) age? Or maybe it's just a throwaway line to begin with and nothing matters. Kaji is one of the few people who ever seems to interact with Mari, and it's a pretty short list: Kaji at the start of 2.0, Shinji later in the same movie, Ritsuko during the latter half of the film (though granted, only in the capacity of knowing that Mari is in Unit 02 and that she's refusing to talk to headquarters) and then in Q, Misato as her commanding officer, Asuka as... an unspecified relationship, and Rei, in the context of communicating with her through the Eva's comms. Then finally she talks with Fuyutsuki in Shin. Interestingly enough, the number of people we actually see physically interact with Mari is very small. We can infer she comes into contact with people when she has to board an Eva, but her only on-screen interactions are with Shinji, Asuka, and Fuyutsuki. Every other interaction is either through an Evangelion, or through someone understanding that she exists in a particular place but not actually seeing her. The more I think about Mari, the more cryptic everything gets.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by bogusman » Fri Apr 16, 2021 12:19 pm Ikr I've thought of something's a bit unsettling about her when back then when i watched. I thought it'll going to be revealed in 3.0+1.0, but instead there are more questions left to answer. And the way she thanked all evas, is like she's the host of the show or some sorta.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Fri Apr 16, 2021 3:28 pm I'm not sure she can work outside of metatext. She's everything Eva never made room for and is in retrospect the source for all major thematic and stylistic differences from NGE. She's both uninvolved and overinvolved in terms of having simultaneously the most distant & intimate relationship with everything going on. There's plenty of questions that remain, but they sort of answer themselves because her function is very assertively 'I'm a battle babe, that's good enough,' or 'this is a happy ending, that's good enough.' She's as enigmatic as she is straight-forward.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by bogusman » Fri Apr 16, 2021 6:52 pm At some point somehow i thought her existence is like "charles"or "marcee" in A Beautiful Mind movie

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 19, 2021 6:17 am That's one of the points that rly irritates me. (& along with Asuka's line & the similar surnames, makes me believe Mari is not the same person, but a clone - cause else it seems inconsistent; Even if it took them a while to figure out that character the least they could do is build on what little they already established, right? Plus she looks 30 in the epilogue not 60. Though I recognize that this is a fanwank at this point. The biggest contraindication is that she calls Fuyutsuki "professor".) Because she doesn't say "people your age" or something like that, she literally says 'adults' as a group separate from herself in the exact way that, say, Shinji does. From that, & her complaining that her new suit didn't arrive in time & the old one doesn't fit, everything would suggest she's exactly as old as she looks. What's especially glaring is that the narrative presents it as an "establishing moment" - Kaji comments on her personality after her first scene as if the line is there to tell us how to parse her actions - "She triggered the self-destruct without a moment's hesitation" (ie, though she acts silly & eccentric she is extremely brave & determined) and then it cuts to her. The line seems to show us an anti-authoritarian streak, she does what she wants & doesn't care for grownups supervising her, she is self-directed, too, as she has "her own goals". If you look at only 2.0 or even Q, it seems consistent enough: Thanking the EVAs shows how she known they're living beings. She has lots of hidden knowledge that allows her to sneak into NERV and trigger beast mode etc, or "connections" as Asuka puts it. She is also somewhat aloof, though NOT aggressively standoffish: She talks about how she'd rather not rely on Kaji, complains to her allies on the phone after crashing into Shinji, she sets the language to Japanese in an anglophone base so no one understands her and there is another explicitly lampshaded scene in 2.0 about how she cut the communications - "whoever it is wants to do it alone" (which is why I'm sat we never saw her & Misato getting to know each other; I feel there would have been friction at first) Even in Q, she doesn't wear a WILLE Emblem when all the other members do (Asuka having a blue strip on the collar of her second suit while Mari does not) and saying "whoha they're in deep trouble" when she sees the Wunder fighting the Mk 9 - saying "they" not "we" In TuaT she seems to speak of "adults" as something with a positive connotation, commending Shinji on acting (and smelling^^°) more mature. Of course her opinions could've changed over time but it makes little sense that she would have that line in 2.0 if she would think of herself as an adult already. I'll always defend "gardener" style writing but that requires that you actually build on what you wrote before not just ignore it. That just makes me feel like a dupe for getting invested in something the makers didn't care about as one of the few who actually liked & bothered to analyze Mari in 2.0.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by The18°angel » Mon Apr 19, 2021 12:30 pm Kendrix#920675  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 19, 2021 2:38 pm The18°angel wrote: [spoiler="Kendrix#920675"] a question the curse of the Eva, does it affect the brains of the pilots? or are they mentally adults with the body of a teenager

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Apr 20, 2021 3:39 am Kendrix wrote: The18°angel#920707 wrote: That has never been clarified & it would probably depend on how exactly the biological specifics work. Or indeed if you could even look at maturity as something entirely biological & not much more to do with experience. Though all the interviews seem to refer to the pilots as mentally older & imho they act like e that too though of course other have had other opinions - which to me seem to rely on inflated ideas of the so called maturity of the average person over 18. I've seen super discerning politically literate 15 year olds & super childish 70 year olds. There are plenty of say, hormone disorders that keep ppl from normally progressing through puberty without necessarily halting the "becoming a reasonable adult" part.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by bogusman » Wed Apr 21, 2021 10:45 pm Tbh i really like Mari since it was first introduced, her designs, her looks & personality. One of my favourite character, although new, and hoping that i could finally find a "hero" out of those eva pilots. A new character to explore and as an alternative out of those 3 pilots, would be fun and exciting to see some development about the characters ,relationship with others and “surprises” about her past and what she really is. But it was kinda let down coz in the ending somehow it raises more questions than answers instead. One of the things that still bothers me are her existence in the past and her attachment to Asuka. It was so intimate, eager to let Asuka told her feelings to Shinji, and she was also in Asuka’s instrumentality after Shinji confess. And while the ending seems happy, i still don’t know who or what is Shinji’s holding hand with when they comes out to the real world.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Fri Apr 23, 2021 5:06 pm bogusman wrote: And while the ending seems happy, i still don’t know who or what is Shinji’s holding hand with when they comes out to the real world.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 23, 2021 5:49 pm C.T.1290 wrote: It could be Mari. And it seems that Anno himself supports this pair, if I’m not mistaken.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Fri Apr 23, 2021 6:03 pm ElMariachi wrote: C.T.1290#921045 wrote: It could be Mari. And it seems that Anno himself supports this pair, if I’m not mistaken.

[]: Re: Children, grown-ups, and Mari             Quote Post by bogusman » Fri Apr 23, 2021 8:36 pm C.T.1290 wrote: bogusman#920923 wrote: And while the ending seems happy, i still don’t know who or what is Shinji’s holding hand with when they comes out to the real world.
